articles.title: DON'T LET YOUR BEST IDEAS LANGUISH IN A BACKLOG
articles.summary: Vibe coding was supposed to give anyone the power to bring their ideas to life. In reality: a designer or marketer builds something promising, but engineers still need to spend weeks rewriting it to fit production. More often than not, these ideas never leave the backlog.

articles.title: Get started with v0 by Vercel
HERE'S THE NEW SIZE OF IPHONE 18 PRO'S DYNAMIC ISLAND, PER LEAKER
articles.summary: The iPhone 18 Pro is rumored to keep the pill-shaped Dynamic Island but shrink it by about 35%, reducing its width from roughly 20.8 mm to 13.5 mm, which should be noticeably smaller in everyday use. This redesigned Dynamic Island is expected to be exclusive to the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max.

articles.title: WIX REINVENTS WEB CREATION WITH NEW HARMONY AI WEBSITE BUILDER
articles.summary: Wix has launched Harmony, a new AI website builder that combines human creativity with AI technology through "Aria," an AI agent capable of executing design requests like updating color palettes or redesigning pages via natural language commands. The platform allows users to generate entire sites or specific sections while maintaining full manual control through drag-and-drop editing, with Aria understanding website context to prevent bugs or code breaks. Harmony will roll out to all Wix users over the coming weeks, aiming to simplify web design without sacrificing professional quality or requiring coding knowledge.

articles.title: 'NOT JUST A TYPEFACE': VOLVO'S NEW FONT WANTS TO KEEP YOU SAFE
articles.summary: Volvo Centum is a bespoke typeface designed for in-car displays with a strong emphasis on legibility, calmness, and driver safety. Developed with Dalton Maag, the font is optimized for glance-based reading at speed, aiming to reduce visual noise and cognitive strain while driving.

articles.title: WHY THE NEW WEB REQUIRES A DUAL STRATEGY FOR EXPERIENCE DESIGN
articles.summary: AI bots now vastly outnumber human visitors. Anthropic sends over 6,000 crawlers per human referral compared to Google's 18 bots. Despite lower volume, AI-referred visitors show 17x higher engagement, creating a "Visibility Gap" between shrinking human reach and expanding machine-readable layers. The solution involves creating "human sanctuaries" for high-trust emotional experiences while optimizing machine-readable content to control how AI agents represent brands, preventing misrepresentation.

articles.title: HOW I THINK ABOUT STARTING WITH VARIABLES
articles.summary: Teams often struggle with starting design system variable structures because it feels like setting a permanent foundation, leading to analysis paralysis when considering scale, platforms, and themes simultaneously. A recommended approach is to separate variables into non-interactive buckets (environmental elements such as backgrounds and text) and interactive buckets (behavior-driven elements such as button states and feedback). Clarity matters more than completeness initially, as variable structures only need to provide teams a shared starting point rather than being perfect.

articles.title: IS BILLING YOUR CLIENTS IN ADVANCE EVER OKAY?
articles.summary: Creatives are split on billing clients in advance for unused budget: it can work as a sign of trust and smart cash flow management, but only with clear contracts, defined scope, non-refundability, and expiry dates. Without those safeguards, it often leads to tax issues, scope creep, and strained client relationships, making “it depends” the most common—and wisest—answer.

articles.summary: From simple UI styles to full Design Systems, write code using Styleframe's powerful TypeScript CSS API.

articles.title: HOW THE IDEA OF CONVERGENCE SHAPED THE NEW IDENTITY FOR SINGAPORE'S ARTSCIENCE MUSEUM
articles.summary: Singapore's ArtScience Museum has unveiled a new brand identity ahead of its 15th anniversary centered on “convergence”—the idea that innovation emerges where art, science, technology, and culture intersect—timed with the exhibition, Another World Is Possible. The rebrand goes beyond aesthetics, using a flexible visual system, custom type, and refreshed logo to position the museum as an active platform for imagining and shaping future cultural and societal conversations.

articles.title: BEYOND GENERATIVE: THE RISE OF AGENTIC AI AND USER-CENTRIC DESIGN
articles.summary: Agentic AI represents a fundamental shift from traditional generative AI by enabling systems to autonomously plan, execute, and persist in tasks rather than simply responding to commands. There are four levels of agent autonomy—observe-and-suggest, plan-and-propose, act-with-confirmation, and act-autonomously. This post introduces specialized research methods, such as agent journey mapping and simulated misbehavior testing, to evaluate these systems. Metrics like intervention rates, unintended actions, and rollback rates are essential for measuring trust and accountability.

articles.title: CELEBRATING ANALOG IN A DIGITAL WORLD
articles.summary: Tactile and analog practices reliably experience a renaissance during periods of technological upheaval as people seek hands-on counterpoints to digital dominance. As AI promises speed and bland perfection, creatives are turning to time-consuming analog methods—from cursive writing to vinyl records—to foster original thinking and personal meaning. The resurgence reflects not a rejection of technology but a reconsideration of expanded creative toolsets. The slowness provides space for satisfyingly weird ideas absent from machine learning datasets.

articles.title: TOYOTA DESIGNS ROBOT CHAIR THAT CAN WALK, BEND, AND HELP YOU UP THE STAIRS
articles.summary: Toyota unveiled the Walk Me chair at the Japan Mobility Show 2025, a four-legged robotic chair with AI-powered sensors and LiDAR that can climb stairs, navigate obstacles, and adapt to uneven terrain in real time.

articles.title: ARTIST GOES VIRAL FOR MAGIC 'REAL-LIFE PHOTOSHOP' ART
articles.summary: Milly Bambini has gone viral for her oil paintings that mimic Photoshop's “remove object” effect by flawlessly blending people and objects into their surroundings using precise color and texture matching.
